This throws MODULE_NOT_FOUND if juggler-mcp deps are absent, BEFORE any doMock runs — a hard on-disk dep even though the module code is mocked. Same dep existed pre-a7d7dd1, so it is orthogonal to import style and NOT a regression from this leg. |
| Confirm 999.1113 fix location is correct + separate-package rationale (Scooter) | read docs/mcp/juggler-mcp-server.md:45, docs/NFR.md degradation matrix, juggler-mcp/package.json; Scooter --ask | juggler-mcp is a documented SEPARATE npm package (stdio client-side CLI for Claude Code/ClimbRS, not Cloud-Run-deployed, proxies plain REST to JUGGLER_API_URL). Adding juggler/juggler-mcp to the ci-pipeline npm-ci loop IS the right 999.1113 target since the pool runs jest against that same tree. Version-drift being intentional: not documented. |

| 1 | INFO | juggler-backend/src/mcp/server.js:5 | [Correctness / dependency resolution] The revert to explicit subpath imports (/server/mcp.js, /server/stdio.js) is empirically correct for BOTH currently-installed SDK versions, and the reverted commit a7d7dd1's premise is false. — evidence: Resolved each spec through the package's OWN exports map from both node_modules: bare '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server' -> {Server} (NO McpServer) on both 1.29.0 and 1.27.1; McpServer only at /server/mcp.js; StdioServerTransport only at /server/stdio.js. a7d7dd1 changed imports to bare /server and destructured McpServer/StdioServerTransport (undefined) claiming 'v1.27 exports ./server not ./server/mcp.js' — directly contradicted by the 1.27.1 install. transport.js already used the /server/streamableHttp.js subpath and was unaffected. (confidence high) | None — fix is correct. Sign-off: the leg restores working imports. |
| 2 | WARN | juggler-backend/tests/unit/mcp-server.test.js:32 | [Infrastructure / test topology] This leg's two regression suites cannot pass on the CI Gate until the juggler-mcp dependency-install gap (999.1113) is closed, so the Gate cannot witness the fix as green. Deferring 999.1113 is defensible (separate infra layer, no new regression) but the proof-of-fix is only observable where juggler-mcp deps are installed. — evidence: mcp-server.test.js:32-33 and mcp-protocol.test.js:204-205 call require.resolve('@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/{mcp,stdio}.js', {paths:[juggler-mcp]}) at module-load to compute the jest.doMock key. That resolution throws MODULE_NOT_FOUND if juggler-mcp/node_modules/@modelcontextprotocol/sdk is absent — a hard on-disk dep even though the module CODE is mocked. CI runs juggler jest via `make -C test-bed test-juggler-pool` -> run-suite.sh (`cd $REPO/juggler/juggler-backend && npm test`); ci-pipeline.yml:62-71 installs juggler-backend but NOT juggler-mcp and run-suite installs neither, so juggler-mcp deps are absent on CI. The same require.resolve dep existed pre-a7d7dd1 (bare /server, same paths:[MCP_DIR]) so it is orthogonal to import style and this leg introduces NO new CI regression. (confidence high) | Recommended: sequence 999.1113 immediately before/with this leg (add `juggler/juggler-mcp` to the ci-pipeline.yml submodule npm-ci loop — correct target: the pool runs jest against that same $REPO tree) so the Gate can witness the two suites green. If kept separate, Oscar/telly must verify these two suites in a juggler-mcp-deps-present environment (local/test-bed after `npm install` in juggler-mcp) and record that the CI Gate red for them is the known pre-existing 999.1113 gap, not a regression. Not a blocker for the import fix itself. |
| 3 | WARN | juggler-backend/tests/unit/mcp-protocol.test.js:203 | [Modularity / package boundary] juggler-backend's unit tests reach ACROSS the package boundary into the sibling juggler-mcp package (its source AND its node_modules) to require + mock the sibling's module. This inherent cross-boundary reach is the root cause of the CI dep-install coupling in ARCH-W1. — evidence: mcp-protocol.test.js:203 MCP_DIR = ../../../juggler-mcp; :204-205 resolve the SDK from juggler-mcp's node_modules; :273 (and mcp-server.test.js:67) require('../../../juggler-mcp/index'). Because juggler-mcp is a separate package with its own node_modules (documented at docs/mcp/juggler-mcp-server.md:45 'a separate npm package'), the doMock key must match juggler-mcp's resolved absolute path — forcing the test to depend on the sibling's installed deps. juggler-mcp has no test suite of its own (juggler-mcp/ has no *.test.js). (confidence high) | Future refactor leg (NOT this one): give juggler-mcp its own test suite run in its own package context (juggler-mcp/tests + a test script), so juggler-backend's jest stops reaching across the boundary and stops depending on the sibling's node_modules. Removes the ARCH-W1 coupling at the source. |
| 4 | WARN | juggler-mcp/package.json:12 | [Dependency direction / version management] The two sibling packages float on divergent, stale SDK ranges (juggler-backend ^1.27.1 -> 1.29.0; juggler-mcp ^1.12.0 -> 1.27.1). The separate-package split is intentional and documented; the version DRIFT is not, and it is precisely this 'two packages on different SDK versions' condition that created the false premise behind a7d7dd1. — evidence: juggler-mcp/package.json:12 '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk':'^1.12.0' vs juggler-backend/package.json:30 '^1.27.1'. The two do NOT speak MCP-SDK-to-SDK (juggler-mcp calls juggler-backend's plain REST /api per juggler-mcp/index.js apiCall + docs/mcp/juggler-mcp-server.md:45), so strict SDK PARITY is not required for interop and this is not a correctness BLOCK. But the ^1.12.0 floor is ~15 minor versions behind backend's ^1.27.1, and Scooter found NO recorded decision that the version drift (as opposed to the package split) is intentional. (confidence high) | Future leg: bump juggler-mcp's range to align with backend (^1.29.x), re-lock, and commit both package-locks, removing the version-confusion class that produced a7d7dd1. elmo may also want the CVE-surface note on carrying two SDK copies. |
| 5 | INFO | juggler-mcp/package.json | [Extensibility / redundancy] A single npm workspace hoisting juggler-backend + juggler-mcp into one node_modules with one locked SDK version would eliminate all three of: version drift (ARCH-W3), the sibling-package dep-install CI gap (ARCH-W1 — one install populates all members), and the cross-boundary node_modules test coupling (ARCH-W2). — evidence: No 'workspaces' field exists in juggler-backend/package.json, juggler-mcp/package.json, or a juggler root package.json (grep returned none) — the two are wholly independent installs. juggler-mcp's documented independent-distribution requirement (client-side stdio CLI) is still satisfiable from a workspace member (it can be published/copied standalone). ## Scooter Consult
**Q:** Is juggler-mcp being a SEPARATE npm package with its own SDK version range an intentional documented decision or incidental drift? Any decision on where its deps install in CI/test?
**A (cited):** DOCUMENTED that juggler-mcp is a separate npm package: docs/mcp/juggler-mcp-server.md:45 ('The stdio server is a separate npm package in juggler/juggler-mcp/'), package.json description ('stdio transport, HTTP backend calls'), NFR.md degradation matrix (MCP client degrades independently). It is a client-side stdio CLI for Claude Code/ClimbRS, NOT Cloud-Run-deployed, proxying plain REST to JUGGLER_API_URL — a legitimate reason for its own package/node_modules. NOT DOCUMENTED (Scooter gap): whether the divergent SDK version RANGE (^1.12.0 vs ^1.27.1) is intentional vs incidental, and no recorded decision on juggler-mcp CI dep-install location. No veto relitigated.
